reforms.description: The National Budget for 1979 announced a reduction in subsidies and loans compared to previous years, aiming to phase out support for unprofitable production and increase labor market mobility.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Reducing subsidies may lead to short-term job losses and business failures, impacting overall economic stability and growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The National Budget for 1979 included significant modifications to industrial policy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in industrial policy, moving away from extensive subsidies towards a more sustainable economic model.
reforms.source_quote: In accordance with these guidelines, subsidies and loans budgeted for 1979 have been significantly reduced compared with the last two years.
